title: SEV-2: k8s ingress timeout during Phoenix rollout
body: During the Phoenix rollout, the k8s ingress started returning intermittent 504 responses. The payments api saw p95 latency above 2.4s after the canary rollout reached 25 percent. Initial mitigation was to pause the phased rollout and check api-gateway saturation.

title: Postgres connection pool exhaustion in billing api
body: The billing api exhausted pg connections after a background worker queue spike. Postgres CPU was stable, but active sessions increased because celery task workers retried failed jobs without jitter. The runbook recommends lowering concurrency and checking RabbitMQ consumer lag.

title: Runbook: Kubernetes pod crashloop in production
body: If kube pods enter CrashLoopBackOff, check the deployment events, container logs, configmap checksum, and recent canary rollout. For k8s incidents involving api-gateway, compare p95 latency in Grafana and Prometheus before restarting pods.

title: Runbook: PostgreSQL slow queries and lock waits
body: When postgres reports lock waits, inspect pg_stat_activity, long transactions, and migrations. If pg latency affects payments service, enable read-only fallback and notify the incident channel. Evidence should include query samples and p95 latency screenshots.

title: Design note: terminology governance for platform search
body: SkeinRank profiles define canonical terminology, aliases, and slots. Bindings connect a profile to an Elasticsearch index and pin a runtime snapshot so search, RAG, and agents use stable terminology context.

title: Design note: evidence-assisted alias review
body: Alias suggestions should be reviewed with evidence from docs, incidents, and runbooks. For example, EKS may be accepted as an alias for kubernetes only in cloud platform bindings, while pg should resolve to postgresql in infrastructure incidents.

title: Search relevance issue: users search for kube but docs say Kubernetes
body: Support reported that engineers search for kube drain and k8s ingress, but official runbooks use Kubernetes. The platform_ops profile should canonicalize kube and k8s to kubernetes before query planning.

title: Search relevance issue: pg timeout returns unrelated pages
body: A developer searched for pg timeout and found documentation pages instead of postgresql incident runbooks. Binding policy should prefer database and service slots for platform incidents to protect precision.

title: RabbitMQ consumer lag delayed invoice workers
body: RabbitMQ queues built up after a message broker restart. Celery workers recovered slowly because prefetch was too high. Prometheus showed consumer lag and Grafana dashboards confirmed delayed invoice processing.

title: Runbook: RabbitMQ and Celery recovery
body: For amqp or message broker incidents, check RabbitMQ management, queue depth, celery worker health, and retry storms. If task workers are saturated, scale workers horizontally and verify no dead-letter queue growth.

title: Elasticsearch cluster yellow during enrichment preview
body: The es cluster became yellow while enrichment preview was running against platform_knowledge_base. Elastic indexing latency increased, but search remained available. The runbook recommends reducing chunk size and checking disk watermarks.

title: Runbook: Elasticsearch enrichment job rollback
body: If an enrichment job fails after reindex alias swap, verify the target index, alias state, and previous snapshot version. Rollback should restore the previous enriched index and clear pending snapshot metadata.

title: Binding model for runtime search context
body: A profile defines terminology. A binding defines where and how that terminology is applied: index name, text fields, target field, optional filter, timestamp window, and pinned snapshot version.

title: Need evidence before accepting OpenSearch alias
body: A reviewer suggested OpenSearch as an alias for elasticsearch, but the team wants evidence from docs first. Evidence snapshots should show whether OpenSearch appears in search platform documents or only in external notes.

title: Redis cache eviction increased API latency
body: The cache layer evicted hot keys after memory pressure. Redis hit rate dropped and p95 latency increased for api-gateway and payments-api. The fix was to increase memory and review TTL settings.

title: Runbook: Grafana and Prometheus incident triage
body: For latency incidents, check prom alerts first, then Grafana dashboards for p95 latency, error rate, queue depth, and saturation. Attach evidence screenshots to the incident review.

title: Runtime query plan example for k8s pg timeout
body: When a user searches k8s pg timeout, SkeinRank should canonicalize the query to kubernetes postgresql timeout, attach evidence, and generate an Elasticsearch DSL plan with boosted canonical terms.

title: Phoenix search scope should prefer rollout docs
body: Project Phoenix is a platform rollout. Queries like phoenix timeout or phoenix canary should prefer incident reports, deployment docs, and api-gateway runbooks over unrelated project pages.

title: Canary rollout caused API gateway saturation
body: A canary deployment increased edge gateway CPU and caused API gateway saturation. The phased rollout was paused, and platform engineers compared p95 latency before and after the deployment window.

title: Runbook: API gateway incident response
body: If edge gateway latency rises, check ingress controller metrics, upstream error rate, k8s service endpoints, and recent canary rollout. Use Grafana to compare p95 latency and request volume.

title: Snapshot safety for search and RAG agents
body: Runtime search should not use mutable profile state directly. It should use a pinned snapshot so RAG agents, query planners, and enrichment workers all use the same versioned terminology context.

title: Alias suggestion: EKS for Kubernetes
body: Cloud platform users often write EKS instead of Kubernetes in incident notes. The suggestion should be reviewed against evidence before being accepted into the platform_ops profile.

title: Alias suggestion: consumer lag for RabbitMQ incidents
body: Engineers search consumer lag when RabbitMQ queues build up. The phrase should be considered as an alias or related phrase for rabbitmq in the platform_ops profile.

title: Multi-binding search across incidents and runbooks
body: All-docs search can fan out across bindings. The platform_ops binding may query production incidents, while a separate docs binding could query runbooks and design notes with different snapshot policies.
